name: Four Points
description_short: FourPoints Education and Disability Consulting provides individualized support to help people with disabilities navigate independence through education, career, community, and recreation services.
description_long:
FourPoints Education and Disability Consulting, LLC is a Colorado-based consulting firm founded by Shelly Fowler, M.Ed., NBCT, CTRS, an Executive Function & Learning Specialist. The company operates under the mission to "inspire and empower individuals with disabilities to navigate life independently" through tailored interventions and comprehensive support planning.

The company offers four core service areas: Education (navigating IEPs, 504 plans, RtI, executive functioning training, and post-secondary transitions), Career (job readiness, resume building, interview coaching, and employment skill development), Community (public transportation training, safety skills, and resource navigation), and Recreation (adaptive recreation access and therapy-based programming). All services follow a structured four-point methodology: Assessment, Planning, Implementation, and Evaluation with 4-week interval monitoring.

FourPoints distinguishes itself through a highly personalized, strength-based approach that treats each client's needs individually rather than applying standardized solutions. The company explicitly operates from a philosophy that "every individual with a disability can learn to navigate their independence when supported by the right interventions," emphasizing client values and goals throughout service delivery.

This is a legitimate non-profit or professional consulting service that was miscategorized as a "payday-alternative" financial product. It provides no financial services, lending, credit products, or cash advances. It is genuinely positioned as free or low-cost disability support consulting, making it appropriate for the free-help category alongside non-profit counseling services.
services: Individualized Education Plan (IEP) navigation and support
Job search coaching and employment readiness
Adaptive recreation program connection and planning
Ongoing performance monitoring and strategy adjustment
pros: Comprehensive assessment-based approach using surveys, interviews, and observations for individualized planning
Covers multiple life domains (education, career, community, recreation) in integrated service model
Credentials: Shelly Fowler holds M.Ed., NBCT (National Board Certified Teacher), and CTRS (Certified Therapeutic Recreation Specialist)
Regular monitoring with 4-week evaluation intervals and strategy adjustments based on performance
Services include specific vocational support (resume, cover letters, job coaching) beyond just counseling
Recreation therapy component recognizes mental health and wellness alongside skill-building
Free consultation option available by phone
cons: Website provides no pricing information, making cost transparency unclear
Limited information about credentials or organizational affiliations beyond founder's personal qualifications
No details on geographic service area or whether services are available remotely
Website lacks client testimonials or outcome data to demonstrate effectiveness
No information about insurance acceptance, sliding scale fees, or financial accessibility options
best_for: Young adults with disabilities transitioning from school to employment or independent living
Families navigating special education (IEPs, 504 plans) and seeking expert guidance
Individuals with executive function challenges or learning disabilities seeking skill-building support
People with disabilities seeking community integration and recreational therapy support
diagnosis: FourPoints is best for individuals with disabilities and their families seeking personalized consulting support for education, employment, community access, and recreation—not a financial services provider. The company was significantly miscategorized; it provides non-profit-style disability consulting similar to free-help services, not payday lending alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
